itinerary.mapNote: Multi-day approach crossing Zatrwa La pass and descending into the Hinku valley. Remote and wild terrain.
Steep glacier ascent to high camp. Spectacular ridge position with Everest, Cho Oyu, Kangchenjunga visible.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: High Camp → Summit → Khare
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine start. Broad snow summit with unobstructed 360° Himalayan panorama. Descend on glacier to Khare.
Retrace via Zatrwa La back to Lukla. Dramatic exit from the remote Hinku valley.

waypoints.name: Mera Peak Summit
waypoints.note: Highest trekking peak in Nepal. On clear days: Everest, Lhotse, Cho Oyu, Makalu, and Kangchenjunga all visible.

waypoints.name: Zatrwa La Pass
waypoints.note: Key pass linking Lukla to the Hinku valley. First challenge of the route.

waypoints.name: Mera La
waypoints.note: Glacier col. Stunning camp position with evening alpenglow on surrounding 8000m peaks.

itinerary.mapNote: Multi-day approach via Namche Bazaar, Tengboche, Dingboche. Khumbu valley classic teahouse trail.
Short acclimatisation stage up the Imja Khola valley. Chhukung Ri optional acclimatisation hike.

itinerary.route: Chhukung to Island Peak Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Transition to tent camping. Glacier views begin. Equipment check and crampon fitting at camp.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: Base Camp → Summit → Chhukung
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine start ~2am. Fixed rope on summit headwall. 360° views of Lhotse, Nuptse, Makalu. Descend to Chhukung.
Return through Khumbu valley. Celebration dinner in Namche or Lukla.

waypoints.name: Island Peak Summit
waypoints.note: Himalayan summit above Chhukung. Fixed ropes on headwall. Lhotse-Nuptse wall dominates the horizon.

waypoints.name: Chhukung Ri
waypoints.note: Classic acclimatisation peak. Outstanding view of Island Peak, Lhotse, and Ama Dablam.

waypoints.name: Imja Tse Glacier
waypoints.note: Glacier approach to base camp. Crampons required from here.

waypoints.name: Namche Bazaar
waypoints.note: Gateway to the Khumbu. Rest day recommended. Trekking permits checked.

itinerary.mapNote: Transfer from Leh (3500m). Acclimatisation day essential after arrival. Visit Stok Palace. Gear check.
Trek through Stok Valley past Changma meadows. Dramatic entry into the high Ladakhi landscape. Camp established.
Climb high and sleep low. Acclimatisation walk toward high camp direction. Crampon practice on snow patches.
Carry summit packs to high camp at 5600m. Afternoon rest for alpine start. Snow and scree terrain.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: High Camp → Summit → Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine start 1–2am. Crampons on snow slopes to summit at 6153m. Karakoram and Zanskar panorama. Descend fully to base camp.
Full descent through Stok Valley to village. Return to Leh for celebration.

waypoints.name: Stok Kangri Summit
waypoints.note: India's most popular 6000m summit. Snow plateau to triangular peak. Karakoram, Zanskar, Tibetan plateau in view.

waypoints.name: Stok Base Camp
waypoints.note: Main camp for all Stok Kangri attempts. Well-equipped guiding camps in peak season.

waypoints.name: Stok Palace
waypoints.note: Royal Ladakhi palace museum. Worth visiting on acclimatisation day.

waypoints.name: High Camp
waypoints.note: Exposed camp in a col. Cold nights. Alpine start position for summit.

itinerary.mapNote: Classic Khumbu approach through Namche Bazaar, Tengboche monastery, and Dingboche. Strong acclimatisation required.
Pass through Lobuche village. Cross the Khumbu glacier moraines. Base camp below the rocky east ridge.

itinerary.route: Base Camp to Lobuche High Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Technical approach up rock and mixed ground to high camp. Fix ropes on steeper sections. Pump Ri visible across valley.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: High Camp → Summit → Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Technical ridge with fixed ropes and near-vertical rocky sections near summit. Cho Oyu, Everest, and Pumori panorama.
Return via Lobuche, Pheriche, Namche Bazaar, Phakding to Lukla. Final teahouse celebration.

waypoints.name: Lobuche East Summit
waypoints.note: Technical summit with exposed rocky ridge. One of the most demanding Khumbu trekking peaks. Everest, Lhotse, Nuptse and Cho Oyu views.

waypoints.name: Thukla Pass Memorial
waypoints.note: Memorial cairns for lost climbers. Sobering and moving hilltop above Pheriche.

waypoints.name: Khumbu Glacier
waypoints.note: World's highest glacier flows from Everest. Lobuche base camp sits on its eastern moraine.

itinerary.mapNote: Drive from Kathmandu to Besisahar then Bhulbhule (840m). Start of Annapurna Circuit. Marsyangdi River valley.
Classic Annapurna Circuit teahouse approach. Subtropical forest. River crossings and suspension bridges.
District headquarters. Apple orchards. Annapurna II first visible. Manang Valley transition begins.
Upper Pisang base for the peak. Ancient monastery. Annapurna II, III and Pisang Peak dramatically framed.

itinerary.route: Upper Pisang to Pisang Peak Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Transition to tent camping above Upper Pisang. Glacier views. Equipment check and crampon fitting at camp.
Steep approach on snow and rock to high camp at 5000m. Fixed ropes on steeper sections. Night preparation.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: High Camp → Summit → Upper Pisang
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine start 2am. South ridge to summit at 6091m. Annapurna II face fills the horizon. Descend to Upper Pisang.
Rejoin Annapurna Circuit to Manang. Rest day option or continue circuit to Thorong La.

waypoints.name: Pisang Peak Summit
waypoints.note: 6091m on the Annapurna Circuit. Annapurna II (7937m) directly adjacent. Two route options: south ridge (standard) and north ridge (technical).

waypoints.name: Upper Pisang
waypoints.note: Ancient Tibetan-influenced village. Monastery with centuries of history. Best viewpoint for Pisang Peak profile.

waypoints.name: Pisang Glacier
waypoints.note: Northeast-facing glacier on the peak's flank. Crampons from here to summit.

waypoints.name: Annapurna II
waypoints.note: Visible directly from Pisang Peak summit and approach. 7th highest in Nepal.

itinerary.route: La Paz to Base Camp (Zongo Pass)
itinerary.mapNote: Transfer from La Paz (~4000m) to Zongo Pass area. Equipment check, crampon and axe fitting. Short acclimatisation hike.

itinerary.route: Base Camp to High Camp (Campamento Argentino)
itinerary.mapNote: Carry packs to high camp on the glacier. Practice crampon technique on moderate snow slopes. Afternoon rest for summit push.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: High Camp → Summit → La Paz
itinerary.mapNote: Midnight start. Classic triangular snow face to summit at 6088m. Views over Lake Titicaca and altiplano. Descend and return to La Paz.

waypoints.name: Huayna Potosí Summit
waypoints.note: Iconic triangular peak above La Paz. Panoramic altiplano and Cordillera Real views.

waypoints.name: Campamento Argentino
waypoints.note: High camp on glacier. Classic red sunset views over La Paz and Illimani.

waypoints.name: Zongo Pass
waypoints.note: Base camp area. Good altitude for early acclimatisation from La Paz.

itinerary.mapNote: Drive from Quito or Latacunga to Cotopaxi National Park. Short hike from parking lot to Refugio at 4864m. Acclimatisation afternoon.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: Refugio → Summit → Parking Lot
itinerary.mapNote: Midnight start from Refugio at 11pm. Steep glaciated flanks to crater rim. One of world's highest active volcanoes. Descend to parking lot and return to Quito.

waypoints.name: Cotopaxi Summit (Crater Rim)
waypoints.note: Active volcano crater rim. Steam vents visible on clear days. Chimborazo and Antisana in view.

waypoints.name: Refugio José Rivas
waypoints.note: Highest mountain hut in Ecuador. Essential overnight before summit attempt.

waypoints.name: Cotopaxi National Park Entrance
waypoints.note: Entry gate. Park fees collected here. Laguna Limpiopungo nearby for acclimatisation.

itinerary.mapNote: Drive from Huaraz to Llanganuco Lakes trailhead (3850m). Hike to Refugio Perú at 4665m through the Huascarán National Park. Llamas and condors common.
Short carry to high moraine camp at 4900m. Crampon and axe practice on lower glacier. Spectacular cirque with Huascarán Norte towering above.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: Moraine Camp → Summit → Huaraz
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine start at 3am. Glaciated slopes to summit at 5752m. Views of Huascarán, Chopicalqui, and Chopiraju. Descend to trailhead and return to Huaraz.

waypoints.name: Nevado Pisco Summit
waypoints.note: Ideal introductory glacier peak in Cordillera Blanca. Huascarán Norte and Sur dominate the view.

waypoints.name: Llanganuco Lakes
waypoints.note: Stunning turquoise glacial lakes at the trailhead. Chinancocha and Orconcocha.

waypoints.name: Refugio Perú
waypoints.note: Basic hut with gear rental and guiding services available. Good base for Pisco acclimatisation.

itinerary.mapNote: Classic Annapurna Sanctuary approach via Ghandruk, Chhomrong, Bamboo, Deurali. Annapurna South and Machhapuchhre (Fishtail) dominate the skyline.

itinerary.route: ABC to Tent Peak Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Transition to tent camping above ABC. Tent Peak's north ridge visible. Equipment check and crampon fitting.
Steep snow and rock to high camp at 5000m. Exposed position with dramatic Annapurna South face views.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: High Camp → Summit → ABC
itinerary.mapNote: Pre-dawn start. Exposed snow ridge to summit. Annapurna I, II, III, IV and South in close proximity. Descend to ABC for celebration.
Full descent through the Modi Khola valley. Rhododendron forests in bloom during spring. Return to Pokhara.

waypoints.name: Tent Peak Summit
waypoints.note: Climbing peak above the Annapurna Sanctuary. Surrounded by eight-thousanders.

waypoints.name: Annapurna Base Camp
waypoints.note: High glacial amphitheatre ringed by Annapurna I–IV, Hiunchuli, and Machhapuchhre.

waypoints.name: Machhapuchhre Base Camp
waypoints.note: Fishtail peak viewpoint. Often used as acclimatisation stop before ABC.

itinerary.mapNote: Gondola from Azau to Mir station (3500m), then snowcat or hike to the distinctive orange Barrels Huts at 3800m. Initial acclimatisation walks.

itinerary.route: Acclimatisation: Barrels Huts to Pastukhov Rocks
itinerary.mapNote: Climb and descend to Pastukhov Rocks at 4700m. Critical acclimatisation. Saddle between East and West peaks visible.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: Barrels Huts → Summit → Terskol
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine start at 2am. Snowcat to 4700m, then summit push across broad volcanic snowfield. West Summit at 5642m. Descend fully to Terskol valley.

waypoints.name: Elbrus West Summit
waypoints.note: Highest peak in Europe and one of the Seven Summits. Vast volcanic snowfield. Caucasus range stretches east and west.

waypoints.name: Pastukhov Rocks
waypoints.note: Key acclimatisation waypoint. Snowcat support often available to this point on summit day.

waypoints.name: Barrels Huts (Garabashi)
waypoints.note: Iconic orange cylinder huts. Base for all summit attempts on the standard south route.

waypoints.name: Elbrus Saddle
waypoints.note: Col between East and West summits. Critical navigation point in poor weather.

itinerary.route: Mexico City / Puebla to Tlachichuca (Drive) + Piedra Grande Hut
itinerary.mapNote: 4WD truck transfer from Tlachichuca village (2600m) to Piedra Grande Hut at 4260m. 2hr bumpy 4WD road through pine forest and lava fields. Gear check and early sleep.
Acclimatisation hike on lava and snow above the hut. Crampon practice. The vast volcanic cone above comes into scale. Early dinner and sleep by 7pm.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: Piedra Grande → Summit → Tlachichuca
itinerary.mapNote: Midnight start. Crampons from hut. Jamapa Glacier: 1400m of sustained snow climbing to crater rim at 5636m. Popocatépetl, Iztaccíhuatl, Gulf of Mexico visible. Full descent by 4WD to Tlachichuca.

waypoints.name: Pico de Orizaba Summit (Crater Rim)
waypoints.note: Third highest peak in North America. Citlaltépetl — Aztec Star Mountain. Crater with fumaroles. Popocatépetl to the west.

waypoints.name: Piedra Grande Hut
waypoints.note: Stone shelter at 4260m. Basic facilities. Starting point for all Jamapa Glacier attempts. Crucial overnight acclimatisation stop.

waypoints.name: Jamapa Glacier
waypoints.note: Main ascent route. Sustained 40° snow/ice slopes. Crampons and ice axe essential throughout. Begins at ~4800m.

waypoints.name: Tlachichuca
waypoints.note: Base village. Servimont agency provides 4WD transfers and basic equipment. Start and end point.

itinerary.mapNote: Multi-day trek up the Langtang valley from Syabrubesi. Langtang Lirung (7227m) dominates the upper valley. Kyanjin Gompa is a sacred Buddhist monastery.

itinerary.route: Kyanjin Gompa to Yala Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Short approach through high-altitude meadows and moraine. Base camp with views of Yala Peak's rocky south face.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: Base Camp → Summit → Kyanjin Gompa
itinerary.mapNote: Early morning start. Rocky and glaciated terrain to summit at 5500m. Outstanding views of Langtang Lirung, Shishapangma, and Ganesh Himal. Descend to Kyanjin Gompa.
Full descent of the Langtang valley. Bamboo forest and waterfalls below Lama Hotel. Return to Syabrubesi for bus back to Kathmandu.

waypoints.name: Yala Peak Summit
waypoints.note: Ideal first technical Himalayan peak. Rocky summit with fixed ropes. Shishapangma visible across the Tibetan border.

waypoints.name: Kyanjin Gompa
waypoints.note: Ancient Buddhist monastery at the heart of the Langtang valley. Local yak cheese produced here.

waypoints.name: Tserko Ri
waypoints.note: Classic acclimatisation hike from Kyanjin. 360° views of Langtang Lirung, Langtang II, and the Tibetan plateau.

itinerary.route: Manali to Solang Valley / Base of Approach
itinerary.mapNote: Drive from Manali to Dhundi (2480m). Short walk to camp. Gear organisation and team briefing. Beas River valley above Manali.

itinerary.route: Dhundi to Beas Kund Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Trek through high meadows and moraines to Beas Kund glacial basin (3700m). Friendship Peak visible above. Camp setup.

itinerary.route: Base Camp Acclimatisation + High Camp Carry
itinerary.mapNote: Acclimatisation hike toward the glacier. Crampon fitting and ice axe technique practice. Rest afternoon.
Cross the glacier to high camp at ~4800m. Crampons required from base of glacier. Early night for alpine start.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: High Camp → Summit → Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine start 4am. Snow slopes to summit at 5289m. Kullu valley and Himalayan giants in view. Descend to base camp.
Full descent through Beas Kund basin and Solang Valley back to Manali. Celebration in Old Manali.

waypoints.name: Friendship Peak Summit
waypoints.note: India's most accessible glacier summit. Kullu valley panorama. Deo Tibba and Indrasan visible to the east.

waypoints.name: Beas Kund
waypoints.note: Sacred glacial lake — source of the Beas River. Beautiful camping spot with Friendship Peak above.

waypoints.name: Friendship Glacier
waypoints.note: Main glacier approach to high camp. Crampons required. Moderate gradient ideal for first glacier travel.

itinerary.mapNote: Hike from Stepantsminda past Gergeti Trinity Church (2170m) and up steep ridges to the Bethlemi Hut at 3650m. Kazbek glacier visible above.
Acclimatisation hike up the Kazbek glacier to ~4050m and return. Equipment check and rope team practice. Rest afternoon.

itinerary.route: Summit Bid: Meteo Station → Summit → Meteo Station
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine start ~1am. Glacier and steep snowfield to summit at 5053m. Russia–Georgia border runs through the summit. Return to hut.
Full descent to Stepantsminda. Pass the iconic Gergeti Trinity Church again. Celebration dinner in town.

waypoints.name: Mount Kazbek Summit
waypoints.note: Dormant volcano. One of the highest peaks in the Caucasus. Russia-Georgia border summit. In Greek mythology, where Prometheus was chained.

waypoints.name: Gergeti Trinity Church
waypoints.note: 14th-century Georgian Orthodox church perched on a hilltop. Iconic Kazbek backdrop. Visible from Stepantsminda.

waypoints.name: Bethlemi Hut (Meteo Station)
waypoints.note: Soviet-era meteorological station converted to climbers' hut. Crowded in peak season. Spectacular glacier views.

itinerary.route: Pont (Valsavarenche) to Rifugio Vittorio Emanuele II
itinerary.mapNote: Easy approach hike from Pont through alpine meadows and ibex territory. Historic rifugio with excellent Italian cuisine.
Pre-dawn start. Glacier traverse, rocky ridge to Virgin Mary statue on summit. Highest point entirely on Italian soil. Descend full return to Pont.

waypoints.name: Gran Paradiso Summit
waypoints.note: Only 4000m peak entirely in Italy. Bronze Virgin Mary statue on summit. Views across Graian Alps.

waypoints.name: Rifugio Vittorio Emanuele II
waypoints.note: Classic alpine hut with long mountaineering history. Named after the Italian king who hunted here.

waypoints.name: Gran Paradiso Glacier
waypoints.note: Moderate glacier with crevasses. Rope travel standard. Ibex frequently spotted on moraines below.
